ideas.title: The $35 Solar Mesh Node: Is This Really the People's Repeater?
ideas.why_now: A DIY solar Meshtastic node video just hit 33K views with 275 comments, sparking real debate about off-grid mesh infrastructure.
ideas.angle: We break down whether a cheap solar mesh node is practical for everyday hams, what it actually takes to build one, and how it fits alongside licensed amateur infrastructure. Hands-on and honest, not hype.

ideas.title: IARU HF World Championship: A Beginner's Guide to Contest Weekend
ideas.why_now: WRTC and the IARU HF Championship are happening now, with operators already posting prep and strategy discussion this week.
ideas.angle: Most everyday hams ignore big contest weekends or feel intimidated. We show how to dip in casually, hunt WRTC stations for fun, and log a few contacts without going full contest-mode.

ideas.title: RFI on 80 Meters: Finding and Fighting Your Noise Floor
ideas.why_now: Operators are posting this week about S9+20 local noise floors making 80m unusable, a very common and frustrating problem.
ideas.angle: We walk through practical steps to identify RFI sources at home, cheap tools that help, and realistic expectations. Relatable content for anyone whose shack is surrounded by switching power supplies and LED drivers.

sources.label: Mastodon #hamradio, building chokes in the shack

ideas.title: QRP in the Park: What an Almost-Failed POTA Activation Teaches You
ideas.why_now: Multiple operators shared honest posts this week about activations cut short by weather or weak signals, sparking good conversation about failure and learning.
ideas.angle: We flip the script on the highlight-reel culture. Using real examples from this week, we talk about what a partial or failed activation actually teaches you and why showing up still matters.

ideas.title: Student-Built Transmitters on the Air: Ham Radio in the Classroom
ideas.why_now: A university group put a student-built 40m CW transmitter on the air this week, logging contacts with a crystal-controlled homebrew rig.
ideas.angle: This is a great hook for talking about ham radio as a hands-on teaching tool. We discuss how clubs and schools can run similar projects and what getting a homebrew rig on the air actually involves for a beginner group.

ideas.title: Go-Box Builds: What Actually Belongs in a Portable Ham Station Case
ideas.why_now: Operators are actively posting this week about Harbor Freight case builds, antenna fitting challenges, and cable management for portable setups.
ideas.angle: We do a practical rundown of go-box philosophy: what to include, what people always forget, and how to keep it grab-and-go without overpacking. Gear talk grounded in real activations, not shelfware.

sources.label: Mastodon #amateurradio, mobile antenna on a road trip

ideas.title: Coming Back to HF After 30 Years: What's Changed and What Hasn't
ideas.why_now: Several operators posted this week about returning to HF after decades away, asking about antennas, modes, and where to start again.
ideas.angle: We speak directly to the returning ham. What gear is genuinely different, what still works the same way, and how to ease back in without buying everything new. Warm, practical, and zero gatekeeping.

sources.label: Mastodon #amateurradio, 30 years licensed, just got a DMR radio
Mastodon #amateurradio, passing the full license exam after years of saying this year
